title: hieracium saxifragum subsp. guillonianum
intro: Hieracium saxifragum subsp. guillonianum, commonly known as guillon's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of guillon's hawkweed include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to make a medicinal tea, or using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
